title: Ukraine History Tracker Update — Apr 26, 2026
summary: Russia launched one of the largest aerial strikes of 2026 on April 25, deploying 619 drones and 47 missiles with Dnipro hardest hit — 10 killed, 67 wounded. Ukraine responded with unprecedented long-range drone strikes reaching Sverdlovsk and Chelyabinsk in the Ural region for the first time, over 1,700 km from Ukrainian territory. President Zelensky intensified peace diplomacy, offering to meet Putin in Azerbaijan during a Baku visit and discussing security guarantees with the US. A major EU breakthrough unfolded as Hungary's new pro-European PM Péter Magyar removed Orbán's long-standing veto, enabling a €90 billion ($106B) loan package for Ukraine to proceed.

title: Ukraine History Tracker Update — Sat Apr 25, 2026
summary: Russia launched one of the largest aerial strikes of 2026, deploying 47 missiles and 619 drones in an overnight attack across Ukraine. Ukraine's Drones Reach Ural Regions — First-Ever Strikes on Sverdlovsk and Chelyabinsk. President Volodymyr Zelensky, during an official visit to Baku, offered to meet with Vladimir Putin in Azerbaijan — accepting any neutral country as a venue except Russia or Belarus.

title: Ukraine History Tracker Update — Fri Apr 24, 2026
summary: Russian forces killed 4 civilians and wounded 17 others in shelling of the Kherson region.

title: Ukraine History Tracker Update — Wed Apr 22, 2026
summary: The European Union moved toward approving a €90 billion ($106B) loan package to cover Ukraine's military and financial needs for two years. Russian Presidential Press Secretary Dmitry Peskov stated that Putin is ready to meet with Zelensky but only for 'result-oriented' negotiations when agreements are being finalized.

title: Ukraine History Tracker Update — Mon Apr 20, 2026
summary: Ukraine's Main Intelligence Directorate (GUR) claimed successful attacks on two Russian landing ships and a radar station in Sevastopol Bay, occupied Crimea.
